mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output, rising capacity utilization, and improving corporate operating leverage, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO increases month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,200 thousand units indicates a contraction in credit-sensitive construction activity and household formation, which reduces aggregate demand and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST value 1164.0 as of t-2mo is below threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when HOUST is below 1200 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and comparatively slower industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a demand-pull expansion where consumption outpaces supply, boosting corporate pricing power and revenue surprises, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The relative spread between the two series drives the signal and is not captured by either series in isolation.
failure_condition: The month-over-month spread between RSAFS and INDPRO fails to achieve a Rank IC greater than 0.03 for predicting 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) raise the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is dormant in neutral or easing regimes.
failure_condition: In periods identified as monetary tightening, month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are not followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, reducing household income expectations and aggregate consumption forecasts, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending and aggregate demand, leading to negative revenue surprises and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when RSAFS declines by more than 2% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding employment, wage income, and household purchasing power, which drives aggregate demand and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses above 1.0%, liquidity conditions tighten sufficiently to raise the equity risk premium and trigger risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of weeks when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0% over any 3-year rolling window.
